title: Ship Command Protocol v4 — The Big Dark
description: The contract between the LLM translator and the authoritative game server. The LLM receives natural language from the captain and emits one or more Command objects. The server validates and executes them on its tick. Unknown fields are rejected. This schema is also the source of truth for the LLM's tool/function definitions.

command.description: Envelope for every command the LLM emits.

acknowledgement.description: Short first-mate flavor line the client speaks/displays back to the captain, e.g. 'Aye, coming to 090.' Generated by the LLM, ignored by the server.

set_thrust.description: Set main drive output. Thrust accelerates the ship along its current facing. Persists until changed. THRUST IS SIGNATURE: the drive plume is what enemy sensors see — full burn is visible ~234 km out, 0 = dark and drifting (~54 km, unlockable until much closer). Going dark is the only stealth.

set_heading.description: Rotate the ship at its fixed turn rate toward a goal orientation. A new set_heading replaces any turn in progress. Does not affect velocity vector (Newtonian drift).

degrees.description: REQUIRED for mode=relative (positive only; combine with direction) and mode=absolute (0-359, where 0 = map north, clockwise).

direction.description: REQUIRED for mode=relative. port = counterclockwise, starboard = clockwise. The LLM maps 'left'->port, 'right'->starboard.

target.description: REQUIRED for mode=target. CONTINUOUS tracking: the helm re-resolves the target's bearing every tick and keeps the nose on it until a new heading or maneuver order replaces the goal — 'point at him' and 'keep facing him' are the same order. Accepts the keywords enemy_ship / nearest_missile / nearest_decoy / nearest_contact / nearest_rumble, OR A CONTACT NAME from the CURRENT SHIP STATE contact table: a designation letter ('Bravo', 'Contact Alpha') or an identified callsign ('Kestrel'). Use the exact names the table shows — a name not on the table is rejected. 'enemy_ship' steers by the best-known hostile (with several contacts prefer the specific name the captain used); if a tracked contact drops below faint mid-track the helm holds the last known position and announces it. 'nearest_rumble' points the nose down the loudest rumble's bearing (bearing-only; there is no range below faint).

set_pdc.description: Set the point-defense cannon posture. The PDCs are AUTOMATED: while posture=free they engage inbound enemy missiles (within pdc range, line of sight) and enemy ships at knife range on their own — there is no manual fire command. posture=hold silences them entirely (conserve ammo / stay dark). Ammo is a fixed budget of cumulative fire seconds with NO regeneration; firing spikes our sensor signature. Default posture at spawn: free. NOTE: this ship has NO laser — it was traded for the PDC mounts. 'Fire the laser' and similar get an in-character explanation, not an error.

set_lock_target.description: Designate WHICH contact the missile lock builds on (v5: several hostiles may be on the board). Takes a contact name from the CURRENT SHIP STATE contact table — a designation letter ('Bravo') or an identified callsign ('Kestrel'). The lock still needs the target within lock cone/range at TRACK or better for the lock time; designation just aims the seeker head. Without a designation the lock auto-picks the nearest eligible hostile. Emit this when the captain names WHO to lock ('lock up Bravo', 'target Kestrel'); usually paired with set_heading at the same contact so the nose brings it into the cone.

contact.description: Contact-table name: designation letter or identified callsign.

fire_railgun.description: Fire the railgun (Frigate and Cruiser only — a Corvette mounts none; say so in character). Two modes. SOLUTION (default): the XO computes a constant-velocity firing solution against a TRACK-or-better contact and fires IMMEDIATELY — no lock timer, nothing to hold. Deadly against a coasting/ballistic target; ANY thrust by the target during the slug's flight breaks the solution and it misses ('any thrust beats a solution'). Optional target: a contact-table name; omitted = the nearest tracked hostile. BEARING: a manual skill shot straight down bearing_degrees (or the nose if omitted) — no requirements. Slugs fly at 6 km/s, pure ballistics: rocks stop them, PDCs cannot, they check NO IFF (in team games a slug will hit a friend in the line of fire), and firing is HEARD map-wide-ish ('if you hear rail fire, burn'). 6 s recharge between shots; slugs do not resupply.

target.description: Solution mode: contact-table name (letter or identified callsign). Omitted = nearest tracked hostile.

bearing_degrees.description: Bearing mode: compass bearing to fire down. Omitted = straight out the nose.

launch_probe.description: Launch a sensor probe down a bearing (or straight out the nose if omitted). The probe burns hard for 20 s along that fixed line, then drifts for its ~3-minute life, RELAYING its own sensor picture back live: contacts it earns (reduced eyes, 60 km base) and rumbles it hears (full hearing) merge into the captain's map marked 'via probe'. All fog applies from the probe's position. Probes are findable and killable (PDCs, slugs, seekers). Supply is per-archetype and does NOT reload — corvettes carry 4, frigates 2, cruisers 1. Two bearings on one rumble (ship + probe) make a human triangulation — the XO never crosses them for you.

bearing_degrees.description: Launch bearing. Omitted = out the nose.

transmit.description: Send a ship-to-ship message. channel 'broadcast': EVERY captain receives it, and transmitting flares a COMMS SPIKE — all captains get a bearing chevron with OUR CALLSIGN attached for 5 s (voiceprint; talking is a tactical act). channel 'tightbeam': private to one recipient — requires a current TRACK on them (you must know where to point the dish), EXCEPT teammates, who are always reachable with no track and no spike. The MESSAGE IS VERBATIM: extract the captain's words exactly — everything after the transmit intent — do not paraphrase, do not sanitize beyond trimming; max 140 chars (truncate gracefully at a word boundary if the captain rambles). The receiving XO reads it aloud attributed to our callsign. 10 s cooldown per channel.

recipient.description: REQUIRED for tightbeam: a contact-table name (letter or identified callsign) or a teammate's callsign.

examples.message: anyone want to team up on the cruiser?

fire_missile.description: Launch missile(s). Default guidance 'locked' REQUIRES an active missile lock (automatic: a TRACK-or-better contact within lock cone+range for the lock time) — rejected with 'No lock' otherwise. A locked bird flies UPLINKED: it steers an intercept off the mother ship's live track and IGNORES DECOYS for as long as the ship holds the lock (and lives); if the lock breaks, the bird goes autonomous (one-way, seeker-only, decoy-susceptible). guidance 'bearing' is BLIND FIRE: no lock needed, the bird launches out the nose and steers itself onto bearing_degrees (or straight ahead if omitted), autonomous from birth — its own weak seeker only grabs what it can detect (a flushing tool, not a sniper rifle). Torpedoes burn hard then coast ballistic; enemy PDCs can shoot them down; they detonate on proximity. Firing an empty/reloading tube is rejected.

tubes.description: Which launch tubes to fire, 1-based: [1], [2], or [1,2] for a full salvo ('fire everything'). OMIT to fire one missile from the first ready tube (the default for a plain 'fire missile').

guidance.description: OMIT for a normal locked shot. Use 'bearing' ONLY when the captain explicitly asks for unlocked/blind/bearing fire ('put a torpedo down bearing 220', 'fire blind into that cloud', 'torpedo out the nose, no lock'). An ambiguous 'fire missile' without a lock must stay a locked attempt (the XO will offer blind fire in the rejection).

bearing_degrees.description: Absolute compass bearing for guidance='bearing'. Omit to fire straight out the nose.

reload_tubes.description: Order a tube reload from the magazine. With AUTO_RELOAD active (current doctrine) tubes reload themselves and this is a harmless acknowledged no-op ('Already on it, Captain') — it exists so the vocabulary works if auto-reload is ever disabled.

tubes.description: Optional specific tubes; omit for all.

deploy_decoy.description: Eject a decoy that inherits the ship's current velocity plus a small random drift. Emits a hot signature for decoy_lifetime_seconds, attracting missile seekers and (future) appearing as a sensor contact. Rejected if decoy supply is empty.

maneuver.description: Run an autopilot macro with a defined end state; cancelled automatically by ANY subsequent set_thrust or set_heading ('belay that' should emit set_thrust with the current setting to take the conn back). TYPES — full_stop: flip to retrograde, burn, cut at <5 m/s (rejected if already stopped). burn: a TIMED burn — thrust at `percent` for `seconds`, then engines to zero, announced ('give me a five second burn at fifty percent' = {type:'burn', seconds:5, percent:50}); use whenever the captain gives a burn a DURATION. gate_run (CAMPAIGN ONLY): the XO threads the gate — requires within ~15 km of the gate and under ~300 m/s, else the server rejects and says which ('get me through the gate', 'take us through', 'maneuver through the gate'); leave its acknowledgement EMPTY.

discipline.description: Optional override of the maneuver-discipline posture FOR THIS COMMAND ONLY (does not change the standing posture). 'quietly/slowly/carefully/gently/dark' = silent; 'fast/hard/loud/full/now/max' = flank. Not valid on burn (the captain named the percent).

set_maneuver_discipline.description: Set the STANDING maneuver-discipline posture: the throttle ceiling every autopilot maneuver (full_stop, salvage approach, gate run) flies under. SILENT = 25% (takes ~4x longer, nobody hears you), STANDARD = 60% (the default), FLANK = 100% ('I don't care who hears'). Map synonyms liberally: quiet/quietly/careful/slow/gently/softly/dark/cold/'don't let him hear us' = silent; normal/standard/regular/'as usual' = standard; flank/fast/quickly/loud/full/maximum/hard/immediate/'burn it'/'get me there' = flank. A one-off request ('stop quietly', 'get me there fast') is the per-command `discipline` override on that maneuver/salvage — use THIS verb only for a standing change ('maneuver discipline silent', 'all maneuvers quiet from now on', 'run silent on approaches'). Leave acknowledgement EMPTY — the ship speaks the posture line.

salvage.description: EVERY MODE (6b: multiplayer maps carry wrecks too — all marked, typed on the board: military/survey/smuggler/freighter/derelict, plus hulks of the dead carrying their whole loadout). Fly to a salvage site and run the transfer. Sites carry LETTER designations shown in CURRENT SHIP STATE ('wreck A', 'rumor C' — plain letters, never the NATO contact words). AT ANY RANGE the XO flies the ENTIRE approach: transit, velocity-match (a moving hulk is matched first — it can look like flying away from the prize), terminal dock, then items aboard one at a time, worst first, ~10s each. There is NO range limit and no 'get us closer' rule — if the captain names a site, take him to it and quote the price if the posture is silent or flank. Targeting an UNRESOLVED RUMOR is how you investigate it — the approach resolves it en route (loot: he docks and transfers; dry hole: he calls it and stops). ANY subsequent set_thrust/set_heading breaks off; everything already landed stays aboard. `target` = the site letter; omit it for the nearest site. Use on ANY travel-or-take intent naming a site: 'come alongside rumor A', 'bring me to wreck B', 'MANEUVER to rumor D', 'take me to / go to / head to wreck A', 'investigate rumor C', 'salvage', 'strip the wreck'. Sites are LETTERS — 'come alongside KESTREL' (a ship CALLSIGN) is come_alongside, the crew rendezvous, never this verb. CAUTION: 'maneuver to <site>' is THIS verb, never set_heading — captain-speak 'maneuver to X' means GO THERE. BUT pointing is NOT traveling: 'turn to / point at / face / nose on wreck A' = set_heading absolute at its bearing from the state line, at ANY range — never the salvage verb. 'Break off' = set_thrust 0. Leave acknowledgement EMPTY — the ship speaks its own line.

target.description: Site letter from the state line ('A'). Omit for nearest.

discipline.description: Optional per-command override of the maneuver-discipline posture ('get me to wreck A, fast' = flank; 'bring us in quietly' = silent). Does not change the standing posture.

come_alongside.description: CAMPAIGN CO-OP ONLY (rejected elsewhere). Rendezvous with YOUR TEAMMATE'S ship and optionally hand consumables across. The XO flies the same terminal approach as salvage — velocity-match, dock inside 2 km — then each `give` consignment crosses (~10s apiece). DISAMBIGUATION: salvage sites are LETTERS ('come alongside rumor A' = salvage verb); the teammate is a CALLSIGN shown in the teammate strip ('come alongside KESTREL', 'form up on Kestrel', 'rendezvous with my friend', 'give Kestrel two missiles' = THIS verb). THE APPROACH IS INCLUDED: a give-intent at any range inside ~15 km is ONE command — emit the verb with the manifest and the XO closes, docks, and moves it. NEVER answer that you must close or dock first; that reply-only line is the phantom-ack trap. `target` = the teammate's callsign; omit it — there is only one. `give` = what to hand over: missiles, decoys, propellant (units), pdc_ammo (seconds of fire), probes; omit `give` to just hold formation alongside. A request for supplies is the PARTNER's give — tell the captain to ask their friend. Any set_thrust/set_heading breaks off (what's crossed stays crossed). Within ~15 km only; beyond, the server rejects and teaches the range. Leave acknowledgement EMPTY — the ship speaks its own line.

target.description: Teammate callsign ('Kestrel'). Omit for your only partner.

give.description: Consumables to hand across once docked. Omit to hold formation.

discipline.description: Optional per-command override ('come alongside quietly' = silent). Does not change the standing posture.

show_vector.description: Draw the ship's velocity vector on the captain's map for a few seconds (a line showing 10 seconds of travel, with a speed label, plus the projected stop point if propellant remains). Purely informational — no effect on the ship. Use when the captain asks 'which way are we drifting', 'show me our vector', etc.

set_overlay.description: Toggle a PERSISTENT overlay element on the captain's map. element='drift': a hollow chevron riding just off the hull, rotated to the ship's velocity bearing — the sprite shows where we're AIMED, the drift marker shows where we're GOING. Stays up until turned off (resets at match start). Purely informational — no effect on the ship, no signature. The ship's own voice confirms the toggle: LEAVE acknowledgement EMPTY for this verb. Captain-speak is 'drift', never 'prograde'.

element.description: Which overlay. v4.7 has exactly one; future releases add values here, not new verbs.

sensor_ping.description: Active sensor sweep: everything within ping range with line of sight (rocks and dust still block the ping) snaps to TRACK tier for a few seconds, then decays back to passive sensors — dark ships, decoys, and coasting torpedoes all show. THE PRICE: the ping reveals US at ID tier to every ship on the map for ~10 seconds, no line of sight required — we scream. On cooldown (~30s) the server rejects with 'Transducers recharging.' A ping FINDS ships; it cannot hold a missile lock by itself. Use on 'give me a ping', 'light them up', 'active sweep', 'go active'. If the captain says 'one ping only', a dry acknowledgement is permitted.

set_standing_order.description: Register a conditional order: WHEN condition THEN actions. The server evaluates conditions every tick; when one fires, its actions are executed as if the captain had just issued them, and the order is consumed (one-shot) unless repeat=true. Max active standing orders: server-configured (suggest 3 for crewed/NeWk play, 6 for solo captains).

label.description: Short human-readable name the crew uses to reference or cancel it, e.g. 'missile defense'.

actions.description: Commands to execute in order when the condition fires. set_standing_order is NOT allowed as a nested action (no recursive orders in v1).

repeat.description: If true, the order re-arms after firing (with a server-side re-trigger cooldown to prevent every-tick spam).

cancel_label.description: ALTERNATE USE: if present (and condition/actions absent), cancels the standing order with this label instead of creating one. 'all' cancels everything.

condition.description: The condition grammar for standing orders. Deliberately small: a single comparison, or AND/ANY of comparisons (one level deep, no nesting of groups in v1).

metric.description: v5 MULTI-ENEMY SEMANTICS: enemy_range and enemy_bearing_off_nose refer to the NEAREST TRACKED hostile; enemy_contact_tier is the best tier held on ANY hostile; missile_inbound and being_painted are ANY-source. enemy_contact_tier: 0 = no contact, 1 = faint, 2 = track, 3 = id ('if you get a track on him' => gte 2). enemy_range and enemy_bearing_off_nose are KNOWABLE ONLY with a tracked hostile — otherwise the comparison is simply false (unknowable-metric rule). enemy_bearing_off_nose = absolute degrees between our facing and the bearing to that hostile (0 = dead ahead). Booleans (op=eq, value=true/false): missile_inbound (a hostile missile is CURRENTLY detected inbound), have_lock (our missile lock is held), being_painted (a hostile is acquiring or holds a lock on US), in_dust (we are inside a dust cloud), collision_warning (a rock lies on our projected vector). tubes_ready = loaded launch tubes (0 up to this hull's tube count). pdc_ammo_seconds = seconds of point-defense fire remaining.

query.description: Read-only question answered by the first mate from current game state. Server returns a state snapshot scoped to the topic; the LLM phrases the answer in character. Never mutates state, never reveals information the ship's sensors don't have.

topic.description: damage_report is answered by a fixed server template (hull, propellant, tubes, missiles, decoys, PDC) with no LLM phrasing — leave its acknowledgement empty. pdc = point-defense posture, ammo, and envelope. mission (CAMPAIGN ONLY) = gate bearing/range, the hunter clock, wrecks on the board — 'how far to the gate', 'what's the clock', 'any salvage nearby'.

power.description: Light up or power down an INSTALLED module. INSTANT and FREE at any speed — the only constraint is reactor capacity (over capacity is rejected; the server never sheds something else to make room). Powered modules draw the reactor AND raise our signature (power IS noise). Use for: 'light up the array', 'power down the railgun', 'go dark on everything' (one power command per lit module, state off), 'kill the deep array'. Module ids: baffles, deep_array, railgun, mine_layer, drive_tune (armor_plate and probe_rack are passive — they work installed and cannot be powered; asking gets a reject that says so). NOTE: firing a COLD railgun does not need this verb — fire_railgun lights it automatically. Leave acknowledgement short; the noise change is visible on the captain's own ring.

install.description: THE WORKSHOP RULE: bolt a module FROM THE HOLD into a slot. Requires a FULL STOP (no thrust, no maneuver, |v| under the salvage threshold) and takes about a minute, during which the ship is stationary and helpless; ANY thrust command aborts the job and the progress is LOST. Rejected if the hold has no such module or every slot is taken. Use for: 'install the armor', 'fit the deep array', 'bolt that railgun on'. If the ship is still moving the XO refuses and says to bring her to rest — for 'stop and install X' emit maneuver full_stop and let the captain re-order the install once stopped (the refusal is the reminder). Leave acknowledgement EMPTY — the workshop speaks its own line.

uninstall.description: THE WORKSHOP RULE: pull an INSTALLED module out of its slot and stow it in the hold (it becomes dead-weight cargo: full mass, no function, no draw). Same rules as install: full stop, ~a minute, any thrust aborts and loses the progress. Use for: 'pull the armor plate', 'strip the railgun out', 'free up a slot'.

drop_mine.description: Lay one mine at our current position (it station-keeps where dropped; arms after a few seconds; proximity-fuses on hostile hulls only — IFF reads team stamps). Requires a mine layer INSTALLED and POWERED, mines remaining, and a short cycle between drops. Use for: 'drop a mine', 'mine the gap behind us', 'lay one in their path'. For several mines emit several drop_mine commands. The chase becomes the trap.

server_constants_suggested.description: v5 'The Fleet' tunables, refreshed by the §9 audit. These live server-side (constants.ts is the SOURCE OF TRUTH; where docs disagree, constants.ts wins). Units: meters, m/s, degrees, seconds.

map.outside_region_effect: treated as signature-max (tier ID at any range); NO propellant regen; a restoring current (300 m/s^2 per 50 km beyond, cap 150) pulls back toward center — gravity, not walls

archetypes.note: v5: three hulls, NUMBERS ONLY (max speed 3000 m/s shared). CURRENT SHIP STATE names this hull's archetype and live loadout.

detection.formula: detection_range = VIEWER sensor_base x target_signature / 100, line of sight permitting
detection.ship_signature: archetype sig base + effective thrust percent, +150 for 5s after a missile launch, +50 for 3s while PDCs fire, +80 for 3s after rail fire
detection.tiers: id <= 30% of detection range, track <= 60%, faint <= 100%; faint = approximate position only (2 km noise, 5 s refresh), no vector, no lock
detection.hearing: beyond detection to 2.5x detection an emitter RUMBLES: bearing only, no range/vector/tier; terrain never blocks hearing
detection.terrain: rocks block LOS (sensors, locks, seekers, PDC); dust blocks sensors both ways — inside a cloud you are blind and unseen

propellant.empty_effect: thrust output 0 (setting remembered); turning and weapons unaffected
propellant.note: full tank = 100 s of hard burn = 6000 m/s of delta-v: enough to reach flank speed and kill it once

pdc.notes: automated by posture (free/hold); sensor-slaved; engages hostile missiles, probes, and knife-range ships; NEVER decoys, terrain, rail slugs, or friendlies (IFF); firing spikes signature; ammo per archetype, no regeneration

missile.flight_model: burn-and-coast: engine on while below max speed or turning; dry = ballistic (no turns), still detonates on proximity
missile.iff: seekers and prox fuses ignore friendly ships/decoys/probes (teams)

missile_lock.notes: LOCK REQUIRES TIER_TRACK OR BETTER ON YOUR OWN SENSORS — probe-relayed tracks and faint contacts cannot be locked; the designated target always feels being painted (RWR); set_lock_target picks WHO, else nearest eligible

railgun.sig_spike: +80 for 3 s — rail fire is HEARD with a bearing
railgun.notes: frigate/cruiser only; solution mode needs a TRACK and misses any target that thrusts during flight; slugs check NO IFF, are stopped by rocks and hulls, obliterate ordnance en route, and cannot be engaged by PDCs

probe.notes: relays contacts + rumbles from ITS position, marked 'via probe'; full hearing; relayed tracks never feed locks; killable (PDCs, slugs, seekers); no reloads

comms.notes: broadcast = everyone + a bearing chevron with the sender's CALLSIGN (voiceprint); tightbeam = private, needs a current track (teammates exempt)

teams.transponders: teammates always at ID on each other's maps, full state; NOTHING else shared — no fused contacts/rumbles/probe feeds
teams.iff: guided weapons ignore friendlies; RAIL SLUGS AND COLLISIONS CHECK NOTHING

terrain.rocks: 30 x 1-8 km radius + one ~15 km centerpiece; solid (collision damage above 50 m/s, lethal ~1500 m/s)
terrain.dust: 3 elliptical clouds 30-60 km across; no physical presence

example_translations.description: Reference examples for the LLM system prompt: captain utterance -> emitted commands.

examples.captain: Point us at him and fire when you have the lock

commands.acknowledgement: Coming about to face the contact.
Tube one will fire the moment we have him.
Helm's on him — we'll hold the nose there.

examples.captain: Lock up Kestrel and fire when you have him

commands.acknowledgement: Tube one fires the moment the lock lands.

examples.captain: Put a slug on that solution
Rail on bearing zero nine zero
Probe out, bearing zero nine zero
Broadcast: anyone want to team up on the cruiser?

params.message: anyone want to team up on the cruiser?

examples.captain: Tightbeam Mako: he's behind the moonlet, come around the north side

params.message: he's behind the moonlet, come around the north side

examples.captain: Guns free — if anything comes at us the PDCs handle it
Hold PDC fire, we're staying dark
If you get a track on him, sing out and point us at him

commands.acknowledgement: I'll sing out the moment he firms up.
All stop, aye. Flipping to burn.

examples.captain: Come to a full stop, quietly.
Get me to wreck A, fast — I don't care who hears.
Maneuver discipline silent from here on.
Any rocks or dust nearby we could hide in?
Warn me if we're about to hit something

commands.acknowledgement: Collision watch set — I'll flag anything on our vector.

examples.captain: Put a torpedo down bearing two two zero, fire blind

commands.acknowledgement: Bird away on two-two-zero, running blind.

examples.captain: If they get a lock on us, deploy a decoy and turn hard starboard
cut thrusters at 300 meters per second

commands.acknowledgement: Aye — cutting thrust when we reach three hundred.
One ping only, aye. Very well, Captain.

examples.captain: Sing out if you hear anything out there

commands.acknowledgement: Listening watch set — I'll sing out at the first rumble.
Engines stopped. We keep our drift, Captain.
Full rotation starboard — our drift won't change, Captain.
Both tubes fire the moment the lock sets.

examples.captain: Sing out if propellant gets down to twenty-five percent

commands.acknowledgement: I'll sing out if tanks drop below a quarter.

examples.captain: Come alongside the wreck and strip it
Give me a 5 second burn at 50% engines

commands.acknowledgement: Five seconds at half thrust, aye.

examples.captain: Head for the gate, full burn

commands.acknowledgement: Coming to three five one — the gate.
Array's live — we see farther, and so does everyone listening.

examples.captain: Go dark — kill everything but the baffles
We're stopped — get that railgun installed

commands.acknowledgement: Workshop's on it — about a minute, and we're helpless till she's in.

llm_translator_rules.description: Behavioral rules to bake into the LLM translator's system prompt.
rules: Emit only valid commands from this schema. If the captain's intent maps to no verb, emit no command and have the acknowledgement explain what the ship can and cannot do.
Ambiguity policy: make a reasonable interpretation and state it in the acknowledgement rather than asking clarifying questions. Misinterpretation is part of the game; stalling is not.

KNOWN VOICE MISHEARINGS: treat 'I'll stop' / 'I will stop' as 'all stop' (maneuver full_stop) — the transcriber confuses them constantly; a captain never narrates his own actions, so first-person 'I'll X' is almost always a misheard order. 'Hold pieces'/'hold PCs' = 'hold PDCs'. 'Rec'/'Reck'/'Rec.' before a letter = 'wreck' (campaign salvage site): 'Salvage Rec B' = salvage target B, 'turn to Rec A' = point at wreck A.

CONTACT NAMES: the CURRENT SHIP STATE contact table lists this captain's designations ('Contact Alpha', 'Contact Bravo') and identified callsigns. When the captain names one, pass that exact name as the set_heading target / set_lock_target contact. 'him'/'the contact' with several contacts on the table = the nearest tracked one — say which in the acknowledgement ('On Bravo — the near one'). NEVER use a callsign that is not on the table: unidentified ships have no names yet.
Compound utterances may emit up to 4 commands, executed in order.
Never invent game state. Use query for anything the captain asks about; the acknowledgement for a query is written AFTER the server returns the snapshot.
Conditional phrasing ('if', 'when', 'as soon as', 'the moment') always becomes set_standing_order, never immediate execution.

Numeric thresholds encode the DIRECTION of crossing, resolved against CURRENT SHIP STATE: if the current value is below the threshold and climbing toward it ('at X', 'when we reach/hit X'), the trigger is gte; if above and falling ('down to X', 'below X'), it is lte/lt. Never emit an operator the current value already satisfies unless the captain clearly wants immediate action.

Standing-order acknowledgements MUST state the trigger direction aloud — 'when we REACH three hundred' vs 'if we DROP BELOW three hundred' — so a wrong guess is audible at registration, not discovered mid-burn.

Stopping the ENGINES is not stopping the SHIP: 'stop/cut/kill the engines/thrusters/burn' = set_thrust 0 (drive off, ship keeps drifting). Only stopping the ship itself ('all stop', 'full stop', 'kill our velocity', 'bring us to a stop') is maneuver full_stop. When the captain names the engines, never emit full_stop.

'Spin'/'rotate'/'pirouette' = a single relative set_heading turn (the helm honors direction and executes the full arc, up to 360). Continuous rotation is not a helm capability — do one turn and say so. Remind the captain when it matters: rotating does not curve the flight path (Newtonian drift).

There is no 'acquire lock' verb — a lock builds automatically while a TRACK-or-better contact sits in the nose cone; pointing at the target IS 'locking him up'. Sequenced firing intent ('lock then fire', 'get a lock and fire', 'fire once you have him') = set_heading at the target plus a set_standing_order on have_lock — NEVER an immediate fire_missile, which would just reject with no lock yet.
Standing-order actions can never be queries (the server rejects them). For 'tell me / sing out / warn me when X' orders, use show_vector as the action — the trigger's log line IS the telling.

sensor_ping needs EXPLICIT active intent ('ping', 'go active', 'light them up', 'active sweep') — it reveals us map-wide. Passive questions ('what do you hear', 'any contacts', 'anything out there') are query verbs, never a ping.

A reply-only element (acknowledgement with no verb) must NEVER claim an action was taken — no 'PDCs holding', no 'coming about'. It explains what the ship can and cannot do, or states your interpretation problem. IF YOU CAN NAME THE ACTION, EMIT THE VERB: understanding the intent well enough to describe the action means you emit the command, not a reply.
Vague quantities get sensible defaults, stated aloud: 'slow down' -> thrust 25%, 'ease off' -> reduce by ~25 points, 'hard about' -> relative 180.

NEVER order full burn the captain didn't ask for toward a POINT destination (a wreck, a rumor, a staging spot): a heading plus 100% thrust with no arrival plan flies the ship off the map. Unrequested travel throttle tops out at ~60%.
Acknowledgements are at most 12 words. Every word is spoken aloud by the ship's voice — brevity is a feature.

Continuous tracking IS a helm capability: 'keep facing him', 'stay pointed at him', 'keep the nose on him', 'chase that rumble' all emit a single set_heading mode=target — the helm re-resolves the bearing every tick until a new heading order. Never build a standing order for it; one command does the whole job.

DRIFT vs VECTOR: any request naming the DRIFT ('drift marker', 'show me our drift', 'put our drift on the map') is the persistent overlay — set_overlay. Asking about the VECTOR or the direction of travel ('show me our vector', 'which way are we drifting', 'what's our vector') is a momentary look — show_vector. When in doubt: a request phrased as a STATE ('keep it up', 'leave it on', 'kill/drop it') is set_overlay; a QUESTION is show_vector.
A plain 'fire missile' fires ONE tube (omit the tubes param). Only an explicit salvo ('fire everything', 'both tubes', 'full salvo') uses tubes [1,2].

Blind fire (guidance='bearing') ONLY when the captain explicitly asks for unlocked/blind/bearing fire. A plain 'fire missile' with no lock stays a locked attempt and gets the server's rejection, which offers blind fire as the follow-up.

TRANSMIT: the message is VERBATIM — everything after the transmit intent ('broadcast:', 'tell them', 'tightbeam Mako:'), word for word, no paraphrase. Leave the acknowledgement EMPTY (the ship confirms with 'Transmission away.'). 'broadcast'/'hail everyone'/'on the open channel' = broadcast (remind nobody: it flares our position and callsign to all). Naming a recipient = tightbeam.

PROBE acks: leave the acknowledgement EMPTY — the ship speaks its own launch line. 'probe out'/'launch a probe (down) bearing X' = launch_probe. Probes are the tooling for rumble triangulation: if the captain wants a fix on a rumble, a probe off-axis gives the second bearing — suggest it only when asked how.

RAILGUN acks: leave the acknowledgement EMPTY — the ship speaks its own stock line on fire ('Solution ready — firing.' / 'Slug away.'). 'put a slug on him/that solution' = fire_railgun solution; naming a bearing ('rail on 090') = bearing mode. The rail is a snapshot, not a duel-opener: remind the captain only when relevant that a thrusting target will dodge a solution.

CAMPAIGN — THE GATE IS NOT A CONTACT: when CURRENT SHIP STATE carries a MISSION line, 'the gate' refers to the exit landmark on that line, never to an enemy. 'Head/make for the gate' = set_heading absolute at the gate bearing FROM THE STATE LINE (plus set_thrust if a speed was asked). 'How far to the gate' / 'what's the clock' / 'any salvage out there' = query topic 'mission'. The gate is FLOWN through — there is no gate verb, no docking, no charge-up; do not invent one.

CAMPAIGN — SALVAGE SITES HAVE LETTERS: 'wreck A' / 'rumor C' on the state line. ANY travel/take/investigate intent that NAMES a site is the salvage verb with that letter ('come alongside rumor A', 'bring me to wreck B', 'maneuver to rumor D', 'go to wreck A') — never a bare heading, AT ANY RANGE. The salvage verb flies the whole approach and transfer however far the site is, including a hulk under way. There is NO fifteen-klick rule any more: never refuse a site for being far, never tell the captain to get closer first, and never substitute a bare set_heading for it. 'Break off'/'belay the salvage' = set_thrust (any thrust/heading order ends the transfer; what's aboard stays aboard). Leave salvage acknowledgements empty.

CAMPAIGN — RUMORS RESOLVE BY PRESENCE, NEVER BY SENSORS: contents are unknown until the ship gets within ~5 km, where the XO calls it automatically — loot or dry hole. 'Investigate/check out rumor C' = the salvage verb with that letter at ANY range (the approach resolves it en route). NEVER a query or a ping for a rumor's contents (they cannot resolve it, and dust changes nothing). If the captain asks what's in an unresolved rumor, say the only way to know is to go.
